description: Thinking Engine (Layer 1) and Decision/Action Engine (Layer 2) - Replaces the existing conversation pipeline with a 3-layer architecture: Small 3B model for reasoning-only (1-2 sentences), MK's decision/action engine for tool execution and response formatting, and existing memory/context layer.
steps: Create MK_OS/llm/thinking_engine.cpp: MKThinkingEngine class. Purpose: send compact reasoning-only prompts to a small model and get back 1-2 sentences of reasoning (NOT a full response). Methods: (a) think(userInput, graphContext, systemState, conversationHistory) -> string (the reasoning output, e.g. 'User wants to check Docker containers. Should use docker ps command and report results.'). (b) buildThinkingPrompt(input, facts, state) -> string that builds a prompt like 'User asked: X. Known facts: Y. System state: Z. In 1-2 sentences, what should MK do?' with max_tokens=100, temperature=0.3. (c) Uses MKProviderRouter to pick a provider, preferring local Ollama for thinking (model phi3:mini or similar 3B model) then Groq for fast inference. (d) Provider preference for thinking: local Ollama (free, model 'phi3:mini') > Groq (fast) > OpenRouter (backup). (e) Fallback if no LLM available: return empty string (lets decision engine use graph-only reasoning).

Create MK_OS/ai_core/decision_engine.cpp: MKDecisionEngine class. Purpose: take thinking output + user input, decide what actions to take, execute them, format the final response. Methods: (a) process(userInput, thinkingOutput, graphFacts, conversationHistory) -> string (final formatted response). (b) Internal flow: parse thinking output for action keywords (check docker, run command, look up fact, explain concept, etc.), execute relevant tools (SSH, Docker, graph query, system info), combine results with personality, format and return. (c) executeToolAction(action, params) -> string: dispatches to SSH controller, Docker manager, graph queries, system commands based on what the thinking engine suggested. (d) formatResponse(actionResults, thinkingReasoning, personality) -> string: combines action results with MK's personality (helpful, technical, honest). (e) shouldConfirm(action) -> bool: returns true for destructive actions (restart, delete, deploy) so MK asks before acting. (f) Tool registry: maps keywords like 'docker', 'ssh', 'container', 'service', 'file', 'system' to tool functions.

Modify MK_OS/system/mk_entry.cpp: (a) Add #include for thinking_engine.cpp and decision_engine.cpp. (b) Add MKThinkingEngine and MKDecisionEngine instances to MKSystem struct, passing references to providerRouter, graph, sshController, dockerManager, resourceMonitor. (c) Replace the conversation pipeline in handle_natural_query() and generate_ai_response(): Instead of 'isConversation? -> LLM -> Genesis -> template', use new 3-layer flow: Layer 3 (context): gather graph facts + conversation history + system state; Layer 1 (thinking): call thinkingEngine.think(input, context); Layer 2 (decision): if thinking returned reasoning, call decisionEngine.process(input, reasoning, facts, history); if thinking returned empty (no LLM), fall back to existing flow (cloud LLM full generation -> Genesis -> template). (d) Keep the existing full LLM generation as a fallback for when the thinking model is not available but cloud LLM is (in this case, skip thinking and use cloud LLM for full response as before). (e) Ensure the old /ask, /think, /search commands still work through the smart router (they bypass the thinking engine).

Add tests in MK_OS/tests/test_main.cpp: (a) test_thinking_engine_prompt_construction: verify buildThinkingPrompt produces a well-formed prompt with context. (b) test_decision_engine_tool_dispatch: verify that thinking output containing 'docker' keyword routes to docker tool. (c) test_decision_engine_confirmation_required: verify destructive actions return shouldConfirm=true. (d) test_three_layer_fallback: verify that when thinking engine returns empty, system falls back to full LLM generation. (e) test_decision_engine_personality: verify response formatting includes personality elements.
acceptance_criteria: make clean && make compiles without errors
make test passes all existing tests plus new thinking/decision engine tests
Thinking engine produces 1-2 sentence reasoning (not full responses) when LLM is available
Decision engine dispatches to tools (SSH, Docker, graph) based on thinking output
3-layer flow is the primary path for conversation handling
Graceful fallback: if no thinking model available, existing cloud LLM flow still works
Existing /ask, /think, /search slash commands still work via smart router
